files.path: Gemini CLI Subagents  High-Leverage Workflow Playbook.md
files.reason: Pre-distilled file functioning as a playbook on Gemini CLI subagents. Passes strict keep gate on multiple counts (unique tactic set on subagent mechanics + YAML config; non-overlapping framework on three-layer architecture; unique frontier-moves list). Rewritten into operator-first format with Rule/Execution/Example scaffolding per move.

Different product (Deep Research API, not CLI subagents). Every sub-section is tier-1: named parameters (background=True, visualization='auto', collaborative_planning, previous_interaction_id, thinking_summaries='auto'), time-budgeted actions, uncommon strategies (hybrid MCP search, recursive chains, batch + meta-synthesis, dynamic tool gating). Passes strict keep gate on every dimension.

files.path: _archive/Here is Gemini CLI’s March 18th weekly update for.md
files.reason: Versioned release notes (v0.34.0). Release notes go stale. Durable items (JIT GEMINI.md loading, /chat→/resume rename, mcpName standardisation) are folded into Part 1 architecture sections only as relevant. Fails strict keep gate as standalone source.

files.path: _archive/I came accross this post . Please can you extract.md
files.reason: Compressed version of the High-Leverage Workflow Playbook file. ~80% overlap. Every framing, example, and recommendation is more thoroughly stated in the canonical source. Pure duplication; fails strict keep gate (not unique, not non-overlapping, not materially better).

Surface-level Gemini Embedding 2 use-case list (RAG, semantic search, reranking, sentiment, clustering). Textbook embedding 101. For a user with marketing/PPC expertise and an established AI workflow, fails the 'content already known' discard signal.

notes: Iteration 2 changes applied: operator-first format aggressively used in Part 1's 5 moves (Rule/Execution/Example/Edge-case); Part 2 sections stay as bulleted parameter-by-parameter blocks because they're inherently lists. Strict keep gate passed for both kept files. Dedup pass merged 'subagent core insight' bullets from file 3 (duplicate) into Part 1 — no new content from file 3 survived. Contribution_type field on every entry. Size: 7.3 KB (down from iter 1's 8.0 KB) — safely within band; no split recommendation triggered, though split remains the natural future move if either part grows.
